content_en:
Al-Minhaj Sharh Sahih Muslim has achieved the status of the definitive commentary on Sahih Muslim in the sense that later commentators have consistently worked in relation to it — either building on it, supplementing it, or correcting specific points — rather than attempting to replace it entirely. This canonical status, achieved within a generation of its composition and maintained for eight centuries, reflects the extraordinary quality and comprehensiveness of the work.

The immediate reception of al-Minhaj was enthusiastic. An-Nawawi's students and contemporaries recognized it as a uniquely accomplished work, and it began circulating and being taught almost immediately after its composition. Within a century of an-Nawawi's death, al-Minhaj had become established as the primary reference commentary on Sahih Muslim across the Shafi'i world and was widely used in other schools as well.

The commentary's authority has been enhanced by the general recognition of an-Nawawi as one of the most reliable scholars in Islamic history. His combination of rigorous critical standards, comprehensive erudition, personal piety, and clarity of expression makes citations from al-Minhaj authoritative in a way that few other scholars' works achieve. When an-Nawawi says something in al-Minhaj, that saying carries the weight of his entire scholarly reputation.

For students learning to study hadith through commentary, al-Minhaj is often the first major commentary they encounter after introducing works like Riyadh as-Salihin. Reading al-Minhaj teaches the methodology of hadith commentary by example: the student sees how to move from hadith text to linguistic analysis, from linguistic analysis to legal implications, from legal implications to comparative school analysis, and from comparative analysis to resolution. This methodology, absorbed through reading, forms the foundation for the student's own eventual approach to primary sources.

Contemporary scholars continue to publish supplementary studies on specific sections of al-Minhaj, and the work remains in print in numerous editions across the Muslim world. Its place in Islamic scholarship is secure: it represents the highest achievement of the classical hadith commentary tradition and a permanent resource for anyone seeking to understand Sahih Muslim.
